description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly greenish surface, suggesting the presence of moss or algae. The stone has a rough, uneven texture with subtle undulations and natural indentations, giving it an organic appearance. Its coloration is a mix of dark green and gray, with the green tones more prominent on the upper and side surfaces, likely due to environmental exposure. The surface finish is matte to slightly rough, with no visible artificial markings or carvings. The stone appears solid and dense, typical of natural rocks found in urban parks or landscaped areas. Its overall form is somewhat oval, tapering slightly towards one end, and it would serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban setting.
description: A rough, greenish-gray stone with an irregular, oval shape and a matte, mossy surface. It appears dense and natural, suitable for use as an urban barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and rounded, with a prominent green surface and subtle textural variations.
description_view_1: This view shows a slightly more tapered side, with the green coloration blending into darker gray areas.
description_view_2: The stone looks widest here, with the green surface most evenly distributed and the texture appearing roughest.
description_view_3: This side is more elongated and narrow, with darker patches and less pronounced surface undulations.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mottling of green and gray, with no artificial carvings or regular veining; the green appears as natural mossy patches.
attribute_weathering: Rough with moss or algae growth, indicating moderate weathering and exposure to moisture and shade.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with planted or grassy areas, often partially surrounded by vegetation or soil, and visually integrates with other natural elements in the landscape.
